name: Gothic
notes: Gothic (4th c. CE; Wulfila's Bible, Codex Argenteus). East Germanic, the earliest well-attested Germanic language; Visigothic kingdom in Iberia ~470-711 CE left substrate loanwords in Ibero-Romance (guerra, guardar, ropa, yelmo). INPUT CONTRACT: native Gothic-script text (Unicode block U+10330..U+1034A, NFC) — NOT the Latin transliteration (a, b, g, þ, ƕ …). The Gothic alphabet is an ALPHABET built by Wulfila from Greek, Latin and runic models. Digraphs 𐌰𐌹 ⟨ai⟩, 𐌰𐌿 ⟨au⟩, 𐌴𐌹 ⟨ei⟩, 𐌹𐌿 ⟨iu⟩ are single vowel/diphthong graphemes and are keyed as multigraphs. 𐌲 ⟨g⟩ is [ŋ] before 𐌲/𐌺/𐌵, keyed as the multigraphs 𐌲𐌲, 𐌲𐌺, 𐌲𐌵. Voiced fricatives [β ð ɣ] are positional realisations of /b d ɡ/. Conservative Germanic: no High German consonant shift; labiovelars 𐌵 /kʷ/ and 𐍈 /ʍ/. The two numeral-only signs 𐍁 (90) and 𐍊 (900) carry no phonetic value and are deliberately left out of the map.
